    6. Be Your Own Banksy
</h2>

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><em>(aka How To Become A Revolutionary Artist In 3 Easy Steps -- This is a early draft of this module. Email me if you see typos or anything that could make it better for future TMS students.🙏🏾)</em></p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/ZOCAMTLqTaeYG4ntaKNd_marina.jpeg" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/ZOCAMTLqTaeYG4ntaKNd_marina.jpeg" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><h2><strong>"If I didn't start putting my art up in random places, my art would have just sat at home, and no one would have ever seen it. If you wait for people to latch on to what you're doing, you'll be waiting around forever. You might as well cut out the middle man and put it out into the world yourself." -- Banksy</strong></h2><p>Once you've mastered daily practice...<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Once you're finally showing up for yourself without fail...<br class="softbreak"></p><p>You're ready for the next step.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Making waves.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Getting a reaction.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Seeing how the world feels about what you have to offer.</p><p>Don't wait for an official invitation. Don't wait for some gatekeeper to write you back.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Make noise now.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>This is how you find other people, like-minded, to start a revolution with.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>This is how you get your first die-hard fans.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Musicians... Stop begging people to go to your Spotify page like everyone else is doing. Instead, carry your music around and play your "hit song" for people at parties, in cafes, everywhere… Get feedback. Get people's emails. Get invites. Busk at a flea market with your drum machine. Share your infectious passion with people who will one day say, "I knew her before she was big.... With that energy, I knew she would make it."<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Painters… Don't wait for a gallery to hang your works. Display your art in unexpected places. Ask mom-and-pop bookstores if they'll put your canvas up on their wall. Talk to repertory theatre managers. And when you find your perfect spot, put a placard with a description of your artistic vision next to it. With PRICE TAG where all will see it.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>What happens when you start moving like this through the world?<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Energy starts forming around you. Expectancy. Even people who don't like your art can't help but respect how you're showing up. Because finally…<br class="softbreak"></p><p>YOU'RE BEHAVING LIKE YOU ARE YOUR #1 FAN. You are treating your art like it's important. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>If you don't have that kind of enthusiasm... How can you expect others to?<br class="softbreak"></p><p>That's why "DARE #2" is so crucial.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>It's when I dare you to...<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Stop waiting for permission.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>It's when I dare you to…<br class="softbreak"></p><p>"Be Your Own Banksy."</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>"Be Your Own Banksy."</strong></h1><h2><strong>To be part of the 1% of successful artists, you have to start doing things that 99% of regular artists aren't doing. </strong></h2><p>You say you want the world to be less polluted by "content" and blessed with more art. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>Cool. But what are you DOING about it?<br class="softbreak"></p><p><em>"Be your own Banksy."</em><br class="softbreak"></p><p>Be among the revolutionary artists of your community.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Make art waves in your city, your town, or your village.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Start now:<br class="softbreak"></p><h2><strong>1. ONCE A WEEK, SHARE YOUR ART IN THE REAL WORLD.</strong></h2><p>Like this... (<a href="https://www.instagram.com/___criatura___/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@__criatura__</a>)<br class="softbreak"></p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>

<p></p><h2><strong>2. DISPLAY OR SHARE YOUR ART IN A PUBLIC SPACE THAT WASN'T NECESSARILY DESIGNED FOR THAT. DELIGHT PEOPLE.</strong></h2><h2><strong>3. DOCUMENT THE MOMENT. DOCUMENT THE INTERACTIONS.</strong></h2><p>Create a piece of visual art. Put it up somewhere unsuspecting. Photograph your act of "art vandalism". Double points if you make your artwork political.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>If you're a writer… Slip one of your poems to a stranger.</p><p><em>Be your own Banksy.</em><br class="softbreak"></p><p></p>
</div>

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=87433956, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-87433956">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><span style="background-color: var(--clinical);">If you're a photographer… Look at what Tadashi Onishi did in his community.</span></p><p>He shares his street photography by projecting them on city walls at night.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>And live stream the results on Twitch.<br class="softbreak"></p><p><em>Be your own Banksy.</em><br class="softbreak"></p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/Aqe8O3YCQ0y6gpgF3MFR_Tadashi.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/Aqe8O3YCQ0y6gpgF3MFR_Tadashi.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><p>If you're a musician… Since you have to create art for your singles, EPS, and albums... And you have to make videos,... Start making a bigger deal of these visual elements. Don't let it be a quick JPG you slap on as an afterthought—or a lazy YouTube visualizer "promotional tool" thingy. Treat your visuals as the bonafide works of art they should be. Giving even more depth to the music. Just like the best musicians have always done with their visuals. <br class="softbreak"></p><p><em>Be your own Banksy.</em><br class="softbreak"></p><p>Or what if you live out in the middle of nature? Are you off the hook? Not at all…</p><p>Hang your painting on a tree (in a way not to harm it). And then let it get affected by the elements. You're letting Mother Nature paint on top of it. And every day, take a picture of it to document Mother Nature's new brush strokes. Rain. Bird pecks. Dirt. Wind. Share the progress in your IG story with us every day so we can watch the evolution with you. And after a month, take it down and sell this collaboration with Mother Nature in your Shopify store for $800. </p><p>Living. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>Breathing.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>See what I mean?</p><p><em>Be your own Banksy.</em></p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=87434043, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-87434043">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><h1><strong>Doing The Bare Minimum Keeps Your Art In The Shadows</strong></h1><p>When I tell creatives… "Find a way to share your art in the real world…" The same thing happens almost every time. </p><p>They complicate it.</p><p>They make the idea so convoluted they'll have to delay action forever. It's their way of buying time. </p><p>Don't be that person.</p><p>Come up with a simple concept and test out a version of it immediately.</p><p>You've been sharing work digitally because it requires minimal effort.</p><p><em>What's scarier is... Going out into the real world and getting live feedback.</em></p><p>What's more nerve-wracking is... Seeing the whites of people's eyes.<br class="softbreak"><br class="softbreak">Those things will also have 100 times more impact on your future than an online portfolio ever could.</p><p>Creatives study what successful artists have done before them and copy their moves.<br class="softbreak"><br class="softbreak">But unfortunately, by the time it's copyable, it's already lost its impact.</p><p>Successful artists sought what no one else had done before and forged their paths.</p><p>That's what I'm asking you to do.</p><p><strong>To sit with it for longer than 5 minutes before you give up.</strong></p><p>What you've been doing is the bare minimum.</p><p>You throw things up. You're filled with wishful thinking. Hoping that someone will discover you. </p><p>But no one cares about your illustrations on Instagram. #truthlotion</p><p>No one cares about your new single on Spotify. #truthlotion</p><p>No one cares about the writing you share online. #truthlotion</p><h2><strong>NO ONE CARES UNTIL… YOU DO REAL-WORLD THINGS TO MAKE THOSE THINGS FEEL SPECIAL.</strong></h2><p>Static art is quickly becoming the domain of AI.</p><p>Where does that leave you?</p><p>Becoming a creator of dynamic art.</p><p>Modern art lovers are feeling a deep existential need to connect with others. More so now than at any other time in history. They want to be inspired by people who are "tapped in." People who embody the messages in their art. </p><p>Be that person for them.</p><p>Dynamic art = art that makes a statement.</p><p>Dynamic art = art that is living and breathing in the real world.</p><p>When I tell you to "Be Your Own Banksy," I'm not saying you should copy Banksy's style or methods. I'm saying <strong>BE INSPIRED BY HIS APPROACH OF NOT WAITING FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT FOR VALIDATION.</strong></p><p>If inspiring others through art is high on your priority list… </p><p><em>Then inspire them.</em></p><p>Art is not vandalism.</p><p>Art is religion.</p><p>And sharing art with the world is how we express our beliefs.</p><h1>
<br class="softbreak"><strong>Go Beyond Instagram. </strong>
</h1><p>You've been allowing your art to become content. </p><p>You've been allowing yourself to settle for creating wallpaper with no message.</p><p>It's easy to say, "Make art, not content." </p><p>Now, I'm inviting you to DO something about it.</p><p>Go beyond Instagram.</p><h2><strong>1. Display, or</strong></h2><h2><strong>2. "drop off", or </strong></h2><h2><strong>3. have people read or</strong></h2><h2><strong>4. have people listen to your art</strong></h2><h2><strong>where it can have an emotional impact on people— In a way that social media content never could.</strong></h2><p>Photograph or film the process.</p><p>Photograph or film the results.</p><p>Put THAT up on IG. Your online audience will start feeling like something real is brewing.</p><p>You've been thinking too small. You've been focused only on the World Wide Web. But meanwhile, you forgot about the actual WORLD itself! </p><p><em>Go beyond Instagram.</em></p><p>Everyone shares on social media. That's why this practice, in isolation, keeps you invisible.</p><p>Your new MOTTO should be to share your art in the REAL WORLD. Not just online.</p><p><strong>Make your social media a museum for your real-world actions.</strong></p><p>It's hard to generate momentum for interest in your art when you're not doing things in the real world. </p><p>But ironically, the more real-world things you do… The more your social media and overall audience grows.</p><p><em>I invite you to start this loop today.</em></p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>
